_meta.title: Active vs Passive LLM Moderator in 3-Person Chats (Desert Survival)
_meta.status: POPULATED FROM PI DESIGN DOC — review, set the items marked PI_SET, then lock (preregistration.lock()).
_meta.design: Randomized controlled experiment; unit of randomization = triad (group); between-subjects.
_meta.self_report: NOT collected in this study — objective/behavioral measures only.
_meta.notes: RQs/DVs/analysis transcribed from the PI's design document. Behavioral metrics are produced by research_metrics_v2.py (frozen). Items marked PI_SET need a numeric threshold from the PI before locking.

research_questions.RQ1: How does active vs passive LLM moderation change participation inequality in triads? (message/word share, turn-taking balance, Gini on word share)
research_questions.RQ2: Does active vs passive moderation change the rate and resolution of conflict episodes (interruptions, negative phrasing, repair moves, time-to-repair)?
research_questions.RQ3: Which moderator interventions (direct invitation, summarization, timeboxing, norm reminder) predict near-term behavioral change (e.g., a quiet member speaks within 120s), and do these links differ by policy?
research_questions.RQ4: What efficiency trade-offs arise from active moderation (total messages/words, time-to-consensus, moderator share of turns) and do they offset gains in balance/safety?

hypotheses.H1: Active moderation increases participation equality (lower word-share Gini / dominance gap; higher entropy) vs passive.
hypotheses.H2: Active moderation changes conflict dynamics — more repair moves and faster time-to-repair vs passive.
hypotheses.H3: Active moderation increases behavioral inclusion (more balanced contribution from quieter members), with a possible efficiency/autonomy trade-off (more moderator turns, longer time-to-consensus).

condition_notes: Two between-subjects conditions, group-randomized. (The platform also supports 'no_moderator', which is NOT used in this study.)
exclusion_criteria: Groups with fewer than PI_SET total messages (non-engagement threshold — PI to set, e.g. 20).
analysis_plan: Data nested (participants within groups). Primary group-level outcome = word-share Gini: Active vs Passive via group-level t-test or regression. Participant-level outcomes: linear mixed-effects models with a random intercept for group. RQ3 mechanisms: event-aligned windows / hazard-latency models for intervention -> near-term response, compared across conditions. RQ4: compare efficiency DVs across conditions. PI to finalize exact tests, multiple-comparison correction, and power/sample target before locking.
sample_plan: Pilot 8-12 groups/condition (16-24 total) for manipulation + feasibility; main study 25-40 groups/condition (>=20/condition acceptable for moderate effects).
task: Desert Survival: a triad ranks the recovered items from 1 (most important) to 12 (least) and agrees on ONE final group ranking within 15-20 minutes. NOTE (PI_SET): the design doc lists 8 items but states 12 — reconcile the canonical item list before launch.

instrument_lock.metric_definitions: frozen — research_metrics_v2.py (8.5 LOCKED docstrings); PRIMARY Gini = word share.
